title: Post-Quantum Cryptography and Hybrid Migration · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for post-quantum cryptography and hybrid migration and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit native, entropy, interoperability, side-channel, compliance, or production transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for post-quantum inventory and hybrid migration plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Inventory long-lived confidentiality, authentication, signatures, roots, protocols, dependencies, data lifetime, and migration lead time.
Use Go ML-KEM interfaces for key encapsulation with strict key/ciphertext parsing, shared-key custody, failure handling, and hybrid context.
Use Go ML-DSA parameter sets and context-aware signing with explicit key, signature-size, interoperability, and policy tradeoffs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Crypto Go Quantum Threat Inventory · changed case · Quiz Crypto Go Quantum Thre…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz cryptographic engineering team handles case 2168 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate hybrid ML-KEM and ML-DSA migration on inventory, specified combiner, transcript binding, downgrade defense, and rollback; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory long-lived confidentiality, authentication, signatures, roots, protocols, dependencies, data lifetime, and migration lead time.
